title: The Ebon Spire
summary: Master Denia sends the heroes to the Forest Moon of Endor, deep in the Moddell sector, to find the Ebon Spire — Revan's ancient forward base containing Holocrons with navigational data for the Unknown Regions. The sector's hyperspace anomalies make the journey treacherous, and the dark side has grown strong in the ancient ruins beneath the canopy.

gearNotes.reason: Unknown Regions atmosphere. If jungle spores were a gate in Adv1, an alien world with no prior survey data is at least as dangerous.
Wilderness traversal in unknown terrain plus vertical descent into the Ebon Spire itself. Dead trees, ravines, cliff faces.
No civilization, no cantinas, no resupply. A dead petrified forest has zero foraging options. Extended time in the field.
The Descent implies underground with zero ambient light. A dead petrified tree interior has no bioluminescence. Without light you cannot see.
Ancient sealed chambers and mechanisms inside the Ebon Spire. The key to Project Leviathan's location is not sitting on a shelf.
No medical facilities anywhere in the Unknown Regions. Whatever lives in or around the Spire will cause injuries.
Unknown alien environment — identifying hazardous substances before contact. No prior survey data exists.
Scouting unknown terrain from elevation before committing to a route through the Dead Forest.
Interacting with ancient mechanisms inside the Spire. The Web of the Revanchist likely has puzzles or traps.
Cutting through sealed ancient barriers if the Security Kit approach fails.
If the Spire has any tech interfaces — ancient or otherwise — for extracting the Leviathan location data.
Extracting and recording whatever data the Spire contains about Project Leviathan's coordinates.
If ancient security systems in the Web of the Revanchist are scanning for intruders.

scenes.title: The Approach
scenes.readAloud:
The Moddell sector is a graveyard of broken hyperspace lanes. Twice the navicomputer screams collision warnings. Twice Captain Okeefe wrenches the ship through impossible course corrections.

When you finally drop out of hyperspace, the Forest Moon of Endor hangs below you — green and blue, deceptively peaceful. But Denia's eyes are closed, and her hands are trembling.

"The dark side is strong here. Something old. Something that has been waiting."

Varth pulls up Revan's coordinates: a dead patch in the southern hemisphere where the canopy has petrified. No life signs. No energy readings. Just stone trees and silence.
scenes.gmNotes: The Moddell sector is treacherous — hyperspace anomalies make navigation dangerous. Denia senses the dark side immediately. Varth has Revan's coordinates from the Act I data: a petrified forest zone in the southern hemisphere. The landing is rough — dense canopy forces a clearing landing 5 klicks from the target.

npcs.name: Master Denia
npcs.notes: Still recovering. Senses the dark side. Stays with the ship.

passive.name: Weakened Master
passive.description: Currently at half Vitality (4) and single action per round. Recovering over the campaign.

actions.name: Force Guidance
actions.description: Telepathic guidance. One ally gains +1 Power on their next check.

actions.name: Sense Danger
actions.description: Warn of incoming threats. Party cannot be surprised.

exploit.name: Desperate Burst
exploit.description: Once per adventure, unleash full power. All enemies in zone gain [Stunned]. Denia collapses.

threatBuild.notes: Recurring from Adv2. Still weakened. Stays with the ship — her guidance is remote via the Force.

scenes.hazards: Hyperspace anomalies. Dense canopy prevents direct landing.

decisions.choice: Land in a clearing and trek to the target
decisions.consequence: 5 klick hike through living jungle before reaching the dead zone.

decisions.choice: Risk a closer landing in the dead forest
decisions.consequence: Dangerous approach — petrified trees are like stone pillars. Ship could be damaged.

environmentMechanics.name: Hyperspace Anomalies
environmentMechanics.effect: Moddell sector lanes are unstable. Piloting check required or ship takes damage.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Careful navigation. Denia can sense the safe paths via the Force (+1 tier).

environmentMechanics.name: Dark Side Aura
environmentMechanics.effect: Oppressive sensation. Force-sensitive characters feel nausea. Resolve check or [Rattled].
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Non-Force users are less affected. Discipline and focus help.

rewards.narrative: Landing successful. The trek to the Ebon Spire begins.

pacing.openingBeat: The dangerous hyperspace transit. Collision warnings. Okeefe's skill.
pacing.risingAction: Endor below. Denia's warning. The dead zone visible from orbit.
pacing.resolution: Boots on the ground. The petrified forest awaits.

disciplineChallenges.context: Navigating the Moddell sector's hyperspace anomalies.

control.failure: Rough transit. Ship takes minor hull damage. One system offline until repaired.
control.mastery: Clean arrival AND you map the anomaly pattern — future trips through this corridor will be safer. Permanent navigation data gained.

effect.fleeting: Turbulent transit. The ship shakes and groans but holds together. Minor stress damage to the hull plating.
effect.masterful: Clean arrival. Ship undamaged. The anomalies were rough but your piloting kept the ship stable.
effect.legendary: Textbook navigation. You thread the anomaly field so cleanly that you arrive with fuel to spare and a mapped safe corridor for the return trip.

disciplineChallenges.context: Identifying a safe landing zone from orbit.

control.failure: Best option is a narrow clearing. Tight approach — piloting check on landing.
control.mastery: Ideal landing zone AND you identify natural camouflage. The ship is invisible from the air once landed. No risk of Imperial detection.

effect.fleeting: A clearing that might work. Some tree removal needed. Not ideal but serviceable.
effect.masterful: Clear landing spot 5 klicks from target. Good approach route visible through the canopy.
effect.legendary: Perfect natural landing pad — flat rock, sheltered by canopy, near a freshwater stream. The ship is secure and concealed. Easy approach to the dead zone.

disciplineChallenges.context: Scanning the dead forest zone for energy signatures or life signs.

control.failure: Scanners can't penetrate the petrified canopy. The dead zone blocks everything. Must investigate on foot.
control.mastery: Energy reading identified AND sensor pattern analysis reveals the source is artificial — not geological. Someone built something down there thousands of years ago.

effect.fleeting: Static. The dead zone creates interference that muddles sensor returns. Something is down there but you can't define it.
effect.masterful: Nothing alive. But a faint energy reading deep underground — the Spire. Ancient technology, still powered.
effect.legendary: Detailed scan: underground structure, ~200 meters deep, massive energy signature consistent with a Force nexus. The Ebon Spire. You have depth, bearing, and a preliminary structural outline.

disciplineChallenges.context: Resisting the oppressive dark side emanation from the dead forest.

control.failure: [Rattled] until you leave the dead zone or pass a subsequent Resolve check. The dread is constant.
control.mastery: You resist AND gain insight: the dark side energy isn't malevolent — it's a scar. Something terrible happened here long ago. The land remembers.

effect.fleeting: Uncomfortable pressure. Like walking through cobwebs made of bad memories. Manageable but distracting.
effect.masterful: Uncomfortable but manageable. You push through the dread. Mental clarity maintained.
effect.legendary: The dark side washes over you and finds nothing to grip. Your resolve is absolute. The oppressive atmosphere doesn't touch you — and Denia notices.

scenes.title: The Living Edge
scenes.readAloud:
The jungle is alive and hostile. Massive trees form a cathedral of green, their roots creating a labyrinth of natural tunnels. The wildlife is unfamiliar — avian predators with wingspan longer than your ship circle overhead, and something large crashes through the undergrowth to your left.

Then you encounter the Ewoks.

A dozen small, furry bipeds emerge from the canopy — armed with spears, stones, and an attitude that suggests they consider you trespassers. Their leader, wearing a headdress of bleached bones, chatters rapidly in a language your translator can't parse.
scenes.gmNotes: The living jungle before the dead zone. Ewoks are territorial and suspicious but not inherently hostile. Chief Chirpa's scouts have been watching since landing. The Ewoks know about the dead forest — they call it 'The Place Where Trees Cry.' They refuse to go there. With diplomacy, they can provide a guide to the edge of the dead zone. Failed diplomacy means navigating without help and possibly fighting Ewok guerrilla tactics.

npcs.name: Ewok Scout Party
npcs.notes: Armed with primitive but effective weapons. Guerrilla fighters in their home terrain.

passive.name: Home Terrain
passive.description: In the jungle canopy, Ewoks cannot be surprised and gain +1 Evasion.

actions.name: Rock Drop
actions.description: Drop rocks from canopy. Area attack from above.

maneuver.name: Canopy Movement
maneuver.description: Move through the canopy. Invisible from ground level.

gambit.name: Log Trap
gambit.description: Trigger a pre-set log trap. [Hazard 2] in zone.

exploit.name: Swarm
exploit.description: If 6+ Ewoks engage one target, that target gains [Restrained].

threatBuild.notes: Minion guerrilla fighters. Individually weak but overwhelming in numbers in their home terrain. NOT meant to be fought — diplomacy is the right approach.

npcs.name: Ewok Chief Chirpa
npcs.notes: Wears a bone headdress. Skeptical but honorable.

passive.name: Tribal Authority
passive.description: Chirpa's warriors fight with +1 Power when he is present.

actions.name: Command
actions.description: Order warriors to attack, retreat, or set traps.

actions.name: Assess Strangers
actions.description: Study outsiders. Determine if they are predator or prey.

maneuver.name: Rally
maneuver.description: Remove [Shaken] or [Rattled] from all Ewok warriors in the scene.

gambit.name: War Cry
gambit.description: All enemies must Resolve or gain [Shaken].

exploit.name: Call the Village
exploit.description: Summon 20 additional Ewok warriors. Takes 10 minutes.

threatBuild.notes: Chirpa is a diplomat first, warrior second. His value is as a guide-provider, not a combatant.

scenes.hazards: Dense jungle. Avian predators overhead. Unknown fauna. Ewok territory.

decisions.consequence: Diplomatic approach. May earn a guide to the dead zone edge.
Risk Ewok guerrilla harassment. Slower, more dangerous trek.
Terrible idea. Ewoks on home terrain are a nightmare. And they're potential allies.

encounters.name: Jungle Predator
encounters.trigger: If navigation fails or heroes are loud
encounters.description: A large avian predator swoops from the canopy. Talons and beak. Fast and aggressive.
encounters.tactics: Dive attacks from above. Grabs and lifts smaller targets. Loud noise drives it off.

environmentMechanics.name: Dense Canopy
environmentMechanics.effect: No line of sight beyond 1 zone. Ranged attacks beyond short range are impossible.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Close quarters. Melee and short-range weapons preferred.

environmentMechanics.name: Root Labyrinth
environmentMechanics.effect: Massive tree roots create natural tunnels. Easy to get lost. Difficult terrain.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Ewok guide, Survival checks, or Force guidance from Denia.

items.name: Ewok Guide (Paploo)
items.note: If diplomacy succeeded — an Ewok scout who knows the safe path to the dead zone.

rewards.narrative: Passage through the living jungle. Alliance with or avoidance of the Ewoks.

pacing.openingBeat: The jungle is alive and watching. Unfamiliar sounds and shapes.
pacing.climax: The negotiation with Chirpa — guide or no guide.
pacing.resolution: Path to the dead zone established.

disciplineChallenges.context: First contact with Ewoks. No shared language — gesture and tone.

control.failure: The Ewoks remain hostile. Warning shots from the canopy. Must retreat or fight — neither is ideal.
control.mastery: Full welcome. The Ewoks lead you to their village and Chirpa personally greets you. A feast is offered. Complete trust from first contact.

effect.fleeting: The spears lower slightly. Curious chirping. They're not attacking, but they're not welcoming either. Neutral ground.
effect.masterful: The Ewoks lower their weapons. Chirpa approaches cautiously. First contact achieved peacefully.
effect.legendary: The Ewoks are delighted. Your tone, your gestures — something about you resonates. They escort you to the village with what can only be described as enthusiasm.

disciplineChallenges.context: Convincing Chirpa to provide a guide to the dead zone.

control.failure: Chirpa refuses. The dead forest is taboo. The heroes are on their own — dangerous trek without a guide.
control.mastery: Chirpa assigns Paploo AND shares the Ewok oral history of the dead zone — something evil came from the stars long ago, and the forest died where it touched the ground. Lore clue.

effect.fleeting: Chirpa considers. He won't send a guide but he provides directions — gestured pointing and a crude map scratched in dirt.
effect.masterful: Chirpa assigns a scout named Paploo. He knows the safest route to the dead zone boundary. Reliable guide.
effect.legendary: Chirpa assigns Paploo AND provides a gift: a carved wooden talisman that the Ewoks believe wards against the darkness. It may or may not work — but it shows deep trust.

disciplineChallenges.context: Navigating the living jungle without an Ewok guide.

control.failure: Lost for hours. Encounter hostile fauna. Time wasted and resources spent.
control.mastery: Find the game trail AND identify edible plants and clean water sources along the route. No supply concerns for the trek.

effect.fleeting: Slow going through dense undergrowth. You're heading in the right direction but it's taking twice as long as it should.
effect.masterful: Find a game trail that leads toward the dead zone. Good pace, minimal exposure to predators.
effect.legendary: Natural wayfinding instinct. You read the forest like a map — water flow, game trails, canopy gaps all point toward the dead zone. Arrive ahead of schedule.

disciplineChallenges.context: Understanding Ewok customs from body language.

control.failure: Alien social cues. Hard to read. You may commit unintentional offense.
control.mastery: Full understanding AND you recognize a status hierarchy: Chirpa defers to the medicine elder on matters of the dead zone. The elder may know more than Chirpa shares.

effect.fleeting: Basic pattern: food sharing equals trust. Aggressive posture equals challenge. Simple but useful.
effect.masterful: Gift-giving earns trust. They respect strength and generosity. Tone matters more than words.
effect.legendary: Deep cultural read: their entire social structure, gift protocols, challenge rituals, and — critically — the religious significance of the dead zone. It's not just dangerous. It's sacred.

disciplineChallenges.context: Keeping up with Ewoks who move through canopy, not ground.

control.failure: Exhausting trek. [Fatigued] by arrival at the dead zone edge. Physical checks penalized.
control.mastery: Match their pace AND impress the Ewoks with your fitness. They upgrade their assessment of you — warriors, not tourists. Respect earned.

effect.fleeting: Sweating, panting, barely keeping up. The Ewoks wait patiently at each platform. Embarrassing but you make it.
effect.masterful: Match their pace. Efficient travel through the canopy. Arrive fresh and ready.
effect.legendary: You keep up with Ewoks in their own trees. They're genuinely impressed — chirping excitedly. By the time you reach the dead zone, they're treating you as honorary members.

scenes.title: The Boundary
scenes.readAloud:
You see it before you feel it. The living jungle ends in a sharp line — as if something drew a boundary and killed everything beyond it. On one side, green and noise. On the other, petrified stone trees standing like a forest of tombstones. No birds. No insects. No wind.

Paploo stops dead. He won't cross. He points to the stone trees and chatters rapidly, his voice high with fear. He mimes something rising from the ground. Something that pulls you down.

Then you feel it. A cold pressure in your chest. The dark side, thick as fog.
scenes.gmNotes: The boundary between the living and dead forest is dramatic and instantaneous. The Ewok guide (if present) refuses to cross. The dark side is palpable here — all Force-sensitive characters feel it immediately. Non-Force users feel dread and cold. The petrified forest is visually stunning and deeply unsettling: stone trees, fossilized undergrowth, perfect silence. The ground is unstable in places — sinkholes from ancient tunnels below.
scenes.hazards: Dark side emanation. Petrified terrain. Sinkholes. Perfect silence — hearing-based perception fails.

decisions.consequence: The dark side intensifies with every step. Must endure.

decisions.choice: Circle the boundary looking for a safer approach
decisions.consequence: Takes hours. No safer approach exists — the dead zone is uniformly hostile.

environmentMechanics.name: Dead Zone
environmentMechanics.effect: No life. No sound. No wind. Hearing-based Perception is useless. Isolation amplifies fear.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Stay together. Communicate constantly to maintain morale.

environmentMechanics.name: Dark Side Whispers
environmentMechanics.trigger: Each hour in the dead zone
environmentMechanics.effect: The dark side probes for weaknesses. Characters hear whispered voices — their own fears, regrets, and doubts. Resolve check or gain [Rattled].
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Resolve. Force-sensitive allies can bolster others.

environmentMechanics.name: Sinkholes
environmentMechanics.effect: Ground collapses into ancient tunnels. Athletics check or fall 3 meters. Take damage.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Move carefully. Survival check to identify weak ground.

rewards.narrative: The dead forest is crossed. The Ebon Spire rises ahead.

pacing.openingBeat: The boundary. Living on one side, death on the other.
pacing.risingAction: The trek through stone trees and silence. Sinkholes. Whispers.
pacing.climax: The dark side crescendo — can you keep going?

disciplineChallenges.context: Crossing the boundary into the dead zone. The dark side hits like a wall.

control.failure: [Rattled]. The dark side whispers — memories of failure, loss, fear. Personal demons surface.
control.mastery: You push through AND the dark side reveals something in its probing: a vision of the Spire's builders, ancient and alien. Lore fragment gained.

effect.fleeting: The wall of dread hits hard. You stagger. Bile rises. But you force one foot in front of the other.
effect.masterful: You push through the dread. Your will holds. The dark whispers but you don't listen.
effect.legendary: You walk through the boundary like stepping through a waterfall. The dark side tests you and finds bedrock. The whispers fall silent — even the dead forest seems to acknowledge your passage.

disciplineChallenges.context: Navigating the featureless petrified forest. No landmarks.

control.failure: Disoriented. Wander for hours. Sinkholes become a risk. Time and energy wasted in dead terrain.
control.mastery: Direct route AND you identify the safest terrain — avoiding sinkholes, weak ground, and areas where the dark side energy is concentrated.

effect.fleeting: Slow, careful navigation. Every petrified tree looks the same. Compass readings are unreliable. But you keep moving forward.
effect.masterful: Use Revan's coordinates to maintain bearing. Direct route to the Spire. No detours.
effect.legendary: You navigate the featureless wasteland by reading subtle geological clues — mineral deposits, erosion patterns, and the way the dead trees lean toward the Spire. Arrival is efficient and safe.

disciplineChallenges.context: Crossing unstable ground over ancient sinkhole cavities.

control.failure: Fall into a sinkhole. Must be pulled out. Minor damage, major time loss.
control.mastery: Navigate the sinkholes AND discover a partially collapsed tunnel entrance — a shortcut to the Spire's lower levels.

effect.fleeting: Careful footwork. The ground crumbles under one step but you catch yourself. Close call.
effect.masterful: Confident traversal. Read the ground and step where it's solid. No incidents.
effect.legendary: Moving across the sinkhole field like you were born for it. You actually use the unstable terrain to your advantage, traversing faster by reading the compression patterns.

disciplineChallenges.context: Physical toll of hiking through the dead zone. No water, no food sources, oppressive atmosphere.

control.failure: The cold and oppression take their toll. [Fatigued]. Physical checks penalized until rest.
control.mastery: Shrug off the environmental effects AND maintain enough energy to sprint if needed. Full combat readiness despite the dead zone.

effect.fleeting: Shivering. The cold seeps through your gear. Everything aches. But you keep moving.
effect.masterful: Uncomfortable but functional. Your body handles the dead zone's environment without breaking down.
effect.legendary: Iron constitution. The dead zone's environmental hostility doesn't register. You're warm, steady, and ready for whatever comes next.

scenes.title: The Spire Revealed
scenes.readAloud:
Through the last of the stone trees, you see it. The Ebon Spire.

It is not what you expected. It's a massive petrified tree — the largest you've ever seen, trunk wider than a Star Destroyer's bridge — but it's been hollowed and carved. Ancient, geometric patterns cover the bark-turned-stone. A dark opening at the base gapes like a mouth.

Revan didn't build this. He found it. And whatever he found inside changed the course of a war.

As you approach, the geometric patterns begin to glow with a faint, sickly green light. The Spire knows you're here.
scenes.gmNotes: The Ebon Spire is a massive petrified tree that was hollowed out by an ancient civilization (pre-Republic) and later used by Revan as a forward base during the Mandalorian Wars. The geometric patterns are ancient technology — a security system that activates when living beings approach. The entrance is a test: a Force-reactive threshold that probes intentions. Revan keyed it to accept those who seek knowledge, not power. The dark side has corrupted the security since.

npcs.name: Spire Guardian
npcs.notes: Stone and crystal sentinel that emerges from the wall. Tests intruders.

passive.name: Living Stone
passive.description: Immune to [Stunned], [Rattled], [Shaken], and all social conditions. Blaster damage halved (stone body).

actions.name: Stone Fist
actions.description: Massive stone punch. +2 Power. Destroys cover.

actions.name: Crystal Beam
actions.description: Green energy beam from embedded crystals. Ranged.

maneuver.name: Emerge
maneuver.description: Sink into or emerge from stone walls. Teleportation within the Spire.

gambit.name: Quake
gambit.description: Stomp. All characters in zone must Athletics or gain [Prone].

exploit.name: Judgment
exploit.description: If a character passes a Resolve check (T2/P0), the Guardian stands down and permits entry.

threatBuild.notes: Elite guard construct. NOT meant to be fought conventionally — blaster damage is halved against stone. The correct solution is passing the Resolve check (Judgment exploit). Violence works but is very costly.

scenes.hazards: Ancient security system. Force-reactive entrance. Guardian construct.

decisions.choice: Approach with peaceful intent and submit to the test
decisions.consequence: The Guardian emerges and tests resolve. Pass = entry. Fail = combat.
Tech check to disable ancient mechanisms. Very difficult but avoids the Guardian.
Possible but extremely costly. Blaster damage is halved against stone.

narrativeLinks.note: The entrance to the Spire leads to the Descent.

encounters.name: The Spire Guardian
encounters.trigger: If Resolve check fails or heroes attack
encounters.description: Ancient stone construct emerges from the Spire wall. Massive, slow, devastating. Blaster damage halved.
encounters.tactics: Guardian fights defensively — blocks the entrance. It can be bypassed by passing the Resolve test even mid-combat. It WANTS you to pass.

environmentMechanics.name: Force-Reactive Entrance
environmentMechanics.effect: The geometric patterns glow. The entrance reads Force signatures and intentions.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Approach with genuine purpose. Deception doesn't work — it reads emotions, not words.

environmentMechanics.name: Ancient Stone Construction
environmentMechanics.effect: The environment is indestructible stone. No cover to create. No terrain to manipulate.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Fight in the open or submit to the test.

rewards.narrative: The Ebon Spire entrance is open. The Descent begins.

pacing.openingBeat: The Spire rises from the stone forest — ancient, immense, alive.
pacing.risingAction: The patterns glow. Something is assessing you.
pacing.climax: The Guardian emerges. Test or fight?

disciplineChallenges.context: Studying the geometric patterns on the Spire's exterior.

control.failure: Beautiful but incomprehensible. Alien design philosophy beyond current understanding.
control.mastery: Pre-Republic technology AND you identify navigation-related symbols. This structure was specifically designed to hold star charts. Revan came here looking for exactly what the heroes need.

effect.fleeting: The patterns are geometric but non-random. Someone carved these with purpose. Very old — thousands of years at minimum.
effect.masterful: Ancient language fragments. This is pre-Republic technology. Revan was a visitor, not a builder. The Spire is far older than the Jedi.
effect.legendary: Partial translation: the symbols describe a stellar cartography system — star positions, hyperspace routes, and gravitational constants. The Spire is a library. A navigation library.

disciplineChallenges.context: The entrance test. The Guardian probes your intentions via the Force.

control.failure: The Guardian blocks entry. Must try another approach or answer the challenge differently.
control.mastery: The Guardian is satisfied AND impressed. It grants an additional boon: a mental image of the Spire's internal layout. You know where to go.

effect.fleeting: The probing is intense — like having your soul x-rayed. You resist the worst of it but the Guardian isn't fully satisfied. Partial entry — limited access.
effect.masterful: Your intentions register as genuine. The entrance opens. The Guardian withdraws its probe. You are worthy — for now.
effect.legendary: The Guardian doesn't just accept you — it welcomes you. The Spire opens fully. Every corridor illuminates. The structure has been waiting for someone worthy, and you passed.

disciplineChallenges.context: Bypassing the ancient security system to avoid the Guardian.

control.failure: Ancient technology resists modern tools. Can observe but not interface.
control.mastery: Interface established AND you discover the Spire's power source is a miniature kyber crystal array. Still operational after millennia. Worth studying.

effect.fleeting: Scan returns: massive energy conduits running through the walls. The power source is deep below. Still active after thousands of years.
effect.masterful: Successful interface with the Spire's external systems. Power readings, structural integrity data, and confirmation that the internal systems are fully operational.
effect.legendary: Full technical analysis: the Spire is a masterpiece of ancient engineering. Self-repairing, self-powering, and designed to operate for geological timescales. The builders were beyond anything in the modern galaxy.

disciplineChallenges.context: Sensing the Guardian's nature — is it hostile or testing?

control.failure: Reads as purely hostile. Defensive stance recommended. Approaching with aggression will make entry harder.
control.mastery: It's testing AND you sense the Guardian's age — it has been here for thousands of years, testing every visitor. You sense the loneliness of eternal duty.

effect.fleeting: Something deliberate about its behavior. Not attacking — but not welcoming either. Withhold judgment.
effect.masterful: It's a test, not a kill order. The Guardian wants you to pass. Approach with respect, not violence.
effect.legendary: The Guardian is a custodian, not a warrior. It has tested thousands over millennia. Most failed. It WANTS you to succeed — the Spire needs visitors who are worthy.

scenes.title: The Hollow
scenes.readAloud:
Inside the Spire, the air changes. It's cold — bone cold — and carries a smell like old stone and ozone. Your glow rods reveal a spiral passage carved into the interior of the petrified tree, descending into darkness.

The walls are covered in carvings — star charts, hyperspace routes, and symbols that Denia's voice in your ear identifies as Old Mandalorian. Revan used this place as a war room.

The passage descends. And descends. The temperature drops with every level.
scenes.gmNotes: The interior of the Spire is a vertical descent through carved chambers. The upper levels are Revan's war room — star charts, tactical displays frozen in stone. The carvings are a mix of pre-Republic geometric patterns and Old Mandalorian tactical notations. Denia communicates via the Force (limited range — starts to fade as heroes go deeper). The descent passes through three distinct zones: Revan's chambers, the ancient civilization's ruins, and the corrupted deep.
scenes.hazards: Vertical descent. No light. Cold. Unstable ancient stonework.

decisions.choice: Study the carvings as you descend
decisions.consequence: Slower but gathers intel. Star charts may contain useful navigation data.
Faster but misses secondary objectives.

environmentMechanics.name: Total Darkness
environmentMechanics.effect: No ambient light. Without glow rods, all actions at -2 Power. Darkness beyond the light is impenetrable.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Glow rods. Multiple light sources recommended.

environmentMechanics.name: Thinning Air
environmentMechanics.effect: Air becomes thin. Endure check each hour or gain [Fatigued].
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Breath mask eliminates the problem entirely.

environmentMechanics.name: Unstable Stonework
environmentMechanics.effect: Ancient stone crumbles. Random section collapses. Athletics to dodge.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Move carefully. Test footing before committing weight.

items.name: Revan's Star Charts (partial)
items.note: Recorded carvings of Unknown Regions hyperspace routes.

rewards.narrative: The upper Spire is explored. The descent continues into older, darker chambers.

pacing.openingBeat: The spiral passage. Cold stone. Star charts on the walls.
pacing.risingAction: Descent through Revan's chambers. The air thins. The temperature drops.
pacing.climax: The transition zone — from Revan's work to something much older.
pacing.resolution: The heroes pass into the ancient ruins below.

disciplineChallenges.context: Deciphering Revan's star charts carved into the walls.

control.failure: Beautiful but incomplete. Need the Holocron data overlay to make them navigable.
control.mastery: Partial navigation data AND you identify a specific route marked by Revan himself — annotated in his own hand. Personal notes from the Revanchist.

effect.fleeting: Star positions you recognize — and hundreds you don't. The Unknown Regions are mapped here, but the charts need modern calibration.
effect.masterful: Partial navigation data for the Unknown Regions. Supplementary to the Holocron. Several viable routes visible.
effect.legendary: Extensive star chart data — dozens of routes, dozens of systems, and Revan's personal annotations marking which ones he explored and which ones he avoided.

disciplineChallenges.context: Descending the spiral passage safely. Syntherope anchoring.

control.failure: Loose stone. Someone slips. Minor fall damage and a scare. Syntherope catches them.
control.mastery: Controlled descent AND you identify anchor points for the return climb. The exit will be faster than the entry.

effect.fleeting: Careful descent. The syntherope holds but the walls are slick with moisture. Every step is deliberate.
effect.masterful: Controlled descent. No incidents. Professional rappelling technique. The team reaches the bottom safely.
effect.legendary: Textbook descent. You set pitons, manage rope tensions, and guide the team down like a climbing instructor. Everyone arrives at the bottom fresh and positioned.

disciplineChallenges.context: Monitoring air quality and temperature in the deep.

control.failure: Wrong turn. Dead-end corridor. Must backtrack — time lost.
control.mastery: Navigate perfectly AND identify the Spire's internal logic. The structure is a test — each corridor forces a choice. You've decoded the pattern.

effect.fleeting: Careful progress. Some wrong turns but you correct course. The Spire's layout is disorienting but navigable.
effect.masterful: Efficient navigation. Read the ancient markers and follow the correct path. No time wasted.
effect.legendary: You move through the Spire like you've been here before. Every turn is correct, every chamber is anticipated. The structure feels less like a maze and more like a conversation.

disciplineChallenges.context: Recording the star chart carvings with a datapad.

control.failure: Incompatible technology. Need to build a bridge adapter — time-consuming.
control.mastery: Interface established AND the Holocron activates additional Spire systems. A full three-dimensional star map blooms into existence — the Unknown Regions in detail.

effect.fleeting: The Holocron reacts to the Spire. Blue light flickers. Partial data transfer begins but the connection is unstable.
effect.masterful: Holocron interfaces with the Spire's systems. Data flows. Navigation charts downloading.
effect.legendary: Perfect synchronization. The Holocron was designed for this interface. The Spire recognizes it as a key. Every chart, every route, every annotation unlocks simultaneously.

scenes.title: The Dark Echoes
scenes.readAloud:
The carvings change. Revan's tactical notations disappear, replaced by something much older — flowing, organic patterns that seem to move in your peripheral vision. The stone feels warm despite the cold air.

Then the visions begin.

You see yourself — but wrong. A version of you that made different choices. A version that took the easy path, that betrayed, that broke. It speaks with your voice:

"You know this is where you end up. Every choice leads here. To the dark. To me."
scenes.gmNotes: The dark side manifests as psychological attacks — visions of alternate futures where each hero fell to corruption. These are personalized: the Soldier sees themselves committing atrocities, the Grifter sees themselves betraying everyone, the Noble sees themselves becoming a tyrant. This is a Resolve gauntlet — each hero must face their shadow. Failure means gaining a temporary dark side condition that persists into the final chamber. Success means the shadow dissolves and the hero gains clarity.

npcs.name: Dark Reflections
npcs.notes: One per PC. Personalized shadow versions. Not physical threats — psychological attacks.

passive.name: Your Own Worst Enemy
passive.description: The Reflection knows every fear, regret, and weakness. Social attacks target the hero's specific vulnerabilities.

actions.name: Whispered Truth
actions.description: Social attack. Reveals a painful truth. Target must Resolve or gain [Rattled].

actions.name: Show the Future
actions.description: Vision of the hero's worst possible future. Target must Endure or gain [Shaken].

maneuver.name: Shift Form
maneuver.description: Change appearance to someone the hero trusts, then betray that trust.

gambit.name: The Offer
gambit.description: Offer the hero power in exchange for compromise. If accepted, gain [Corrupted] (temporary).

exploit.name: Dissolve
exploit.description: If the hero passes a Resolve check (T2/P0), the Reflection admits defeat and dissolves. Hero gains [Clarity] — +1 Power for the rest of the adventure.

threatBuild.notes: Psychological enemies, not physical. Cannot be fought with weapons — only Resolve.

scenes.hazards: Dark side manifestations. Psychological attacks. Visions of alternate futures.

decisions.consequence: Resolve checks. Success = clarity. Failure = temporary dark side condition.
Still must Resolve, but at -1 Power from denial.

encounters.name: Dark Reflections
encounters.trigger: Automatic — each hero faces their shadow
encounters.description: Personalized psychological attacks. Each hero faces a dark version of themselves. Cannot be fought with weapons — only Resolve.
encounters.tactics: The Reflections exploit specific fears. They offer power in exchange for compromise. The correct answer is to acknowledge the darkness without accepting it.

environmentMechanics.name: Dark Side Nexus
environmentMechanics.effect: The dark side is concentrated here. All Resolve checks are the primary challenge. Force-sensitive characters feel physically ill.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Group support. Heroes can help each other with Persuasion checks.

environmentMechanics.name: Shifting Passages
environmentMechanics.effect: The physical space seems to change — corridors lead to dead ends, rooms repeat. Navigation is impossible during the visions.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Wait for the visions to pass. They are temporary.

rewards.narrative: Each hero has faced their shadow. [Clarity] or [Corrupted] — the consequences carry forward.

pacing.openingBeat: The carvings change. Something older. Something watching.
pacing.risingAction: The visions begin. Each hero faces their shadow.
pacing.climax: The Offer — power in exchange for compromise.
pacing.resolution: Shadows dissolve or linger. The descent continues.

disciplineChallenges.context: Facing your Dark Reflection. It knows everything about you.

control.failure: [Corrupted] — the shadow lingers. -1 Power on Resolve checks until you confront it again. Personal demons strengthened.
control.mastery: You don't just refuse the shadow — you integrate it. Accept the darkness without surrendering to it. [Clarity] is permanent — +1 Power on Resolve for the rest of the campaign.

effect.fleeting: The shadow shows you your worst self. You flinch — but you don't break. The vision fades. You survive, diminished but intact.
effect.masterful: You acknowledge the shadow but refuse it. [Clarity] — +1 Power for the rest of the adventure. The darkness has no hold on you.
effect.legendary: You face your Dark Reflection and name it. 'I know you. You're the part of me I'm afraid of. But you don't control me.' The shadow dissolves. [Clarity] is earned through genuine self-knowledge.

disciplineChallenges.context: Understanding the nature of the visions — are they real?

control.failure: They feel absolutely real. The emotional weight is crushing. Hard to distinguish vision from reality.
control.mastery: Possibilities, not prophecies AND you extract tactical information from the visions — the dark side showed you things it didn't intend to. Future dangers previewed.

effect.fleeting: Something artificial about the visions. They push too hard. Real memories don't feel like propaganda.
effect.masterful: These are possibilities, not prophecies. The dark side shows what COULD be, not what MUST be. The fear loses its teeth.
effect.legendary: Complete understanding: the visions are a test mechanism. They show your fears to see if you'll surrender. Knowing this robs them of all power.

disciplineChallenges.context: Talking another PC through their vision. Helping them resist.

control.failure: Your help feels hollow. The shadow uses your words against them. Must try a different approach.
control.mastery: Your words break through so effectively that the ally's Dark Reflection dissolves completely. They gain [Clarity] without having to face the full test alone.

effect.fleeting: Your voice reaches them. They pause. The shadow flickers. Not enough to break it, but enough to slow it down.
effect.masterful: Your words break through. The other hero gains +1 tier on their Resolve check. A lifeline in the darkness.
effect.legendary: You say exactly the right thing — not generic encouragement, but something personal, specific, and true. The shadow can't compete with genuine connection. Your ally stands tall.

scenes.title: The Nest
scenes.readAloud:
Below the vision chambers, the passage opens into a vast natural cavern beneath the Spire. Bioluminescent fungus clings to the ceiling, providing a sickly blue-green light.

The cavern is inhabited. Giant spider-like creatures — translucent, multi-legged, with crystalline eyes — skitter across the walls. They've been feeding on the dark side energy for millennia, growing large and predatory.

Your glow rods disturb them. Dozens of crystalline eyes turn toward you from the darkness.
scenes.gmNotes: The Nest is a cavern below the Spire inhabited by Knobby White Spiders — Force-sensitive arachnids that have been corrupted by millennia of dark side exposure. They're larger and more aggressive than normal specimens. The heroes must cross the cavern to reach the final chamber below. Stealth is the ideal approach — the spiders are territorial but won't attack unless provoked. However, the glow rods are already attracting attention.

passive.name: Wall Crawler
passive.description: Can move on walls and ceilings. Ignore terrain. Attack from any direction.

actions.name: Venomous Bite
actions.description: Melee. Target must Endure or gain [Poisoned] — 1 damage per round for 3 rounds.

actions.name: Web Shot
actions.description: Ranged. Target must Athletics or gain [Restrained].

maneuver.name: Skitter
maneuver.description: Move on any surface. Cannot be intercepted.

gambit.name: Swarm
gambit.description: All spiders in zone attack same target simultaneously. +1 Power each.

exploit.name: Retreat to Dark
exploit.description: When bright light source is activated, spiders flee for 2 rounds.

threatBuild.notes: Minion fauna. Fast and numerous. Light drives them away — the exploit is the key. Fire or intense light sources clear zones.

npcs.name: Brood Mother
npcs.notes: Massive spider. Size of a speeder. Guards the deepest passage.

actions.name: Crushing Mandibles
actions.description: Massive bite. +2 Power. Can grab medium-sized targets.

actions.name: Mass Web
actions.description: Area web. All targets in zone must Athletics or [Restrained].

maneuver.name: Ceiling Drop
maneuver.description: Drop from above. Surprise attack. +1 Power on first hit.

gambit.name: Summon Brood
gambit.description: Call 6 additional minion spiders to the zone.

exploit.name: Light Aversion
exploit.description: Intense light (fusion cutter overload, fire) causes the Brood Mother to retreat for 3 rounds.

threatBuild.notes: Elite apex predator. 5. Can be driven off with intense light rather than killed.

scenes.hazards: Giant corrupted spiders. Webs blocking passages. Dark side fauna.

decisions.consequence: Stealth approach. Douse glow rods and move in darkness. Risky but avoids combat.

decisions.choice: Light a path and push through
decisions.consequence: Bright light drives spiders back temporarily but attracts the Brood Mother.
Investigation check. There may be a side passage — narrower but spider-free.

encounters.name: The Nest
encounters.trigger: If stealth fails or heroes choose the direct route
encounters.description: Corrupted Knobby Spiders swarm from walls and ceiling. Brood Mother guards the deepest passage.
encounters.tactics: Minion spiders web and bite. Brood Mother drops from ceiling. Use intense light to drive them back.

environmentMechanics.name: Web Networks
environmentMechanics.effect: Webs crisscross the cavern. Touching a web alerts nearby spiders. [Restrained] if stuck.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Cut webs carefully (vibroblade). Or avoid contact entirely.

environmentMechanics.name: Bioluminescent Fungus
environmentMechanics.effect: Provides dim light. Enough to see shapes but not details. Ranged attacks at -1 Power.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Glow rods for full illumination — but light attracts spiders.

environmentMechanics.name: Light Sensitivity
environmentMechanics.effect: Intense light (fusion cutter, fire) drives spiders back 2 zones for 2 rounds.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: N/A — tactical advantage.

rewards.narrative: The Nest is crossed. The final chamber lies below.

pacing.openingBeat: The cavern opens. Bioluminescence. Movement on the walls.
pacing.risingAction: Spiders everywhere. Choose: sneak, light, or fight.
pacing.climax: The Brood Mother blocks the deepest passage.
pacing.resolution: Through the Nest. The Web of the Revanchist below.

disciplineChallenges.context: Moving through the spider nest without triggering an attack.

control.failure: A web strand vibrates. Spiders investigate. Must freeze and wait or face combat.
control.mastery: Silent passage AND you identify the spiders' blind spots. Can lead the entire team through without any risk.

effect.fleeting: Slow, careful movement. One spider turns toward you... then continues past. Heart-stopping moment.
effect.masterful: Silent movement in darkness. Spiders don't notice. Clean passage through the nest.
effect.legendary: You move through the spider nest like smoke. Not a single web strand disturbed. Not a single spider alerted. The team follows in your footsteps exactly.

disciplineChallenges.context: Identifying the spiders' behavior — threats vs territorial displays.

control.failure: They all look aggressive. Can't tell defensive from predatory. Treat every spider as a threat.
control.mastery: Behavioral patterns identified AND you notice the spiders avoid one specific corridor — something worse than them lives deeper. Navigate around it.

effect.fleeting: Some spiders are bigger, some smaller. The bigger ones seem less agitated. Territorial hierarchy maybe.
effect.masterful: They're territorial but not hunting. Avoid their web-centers and you're safe. Stick to the edges.
effect.legendary: Complete behavioral map: hunting patterns, feeding schedule, territorial boundaries, and the fact that they're nocturnal — which means you're in their sleep cycle. Perfect timing.

disciplineChallenges.context: Breaking free from web restraints if caught.

control.failure: Webbed tighter. Need help from an ally. The more you struggle, the worse it gets.
control.mastery: Break free AND rip a section of webbing that can be repurposed as crude rope. The spiders' own silk becomes useful gear.

effect.fleeting: Strain against the webbing. One arm comes free. Still partially restrained but you can act.
effect.masterful: Tear free. Full movement restored. The webbing snaps like cord.
effect.legendary: Explosive breakout. The web restraints shred. You're free, standing, and ready to fight or run before the spiders can react.

disciplineChallenges.context: Finding an alternate route that bypasses the main nest.

control.failure: No alternate route visible. Must go through the main nest. Stealth is the only option.
control.mastery: Alternate route AND it leads past what appears to be an ancient storage chamber — pre-Republic supplies, still sealed. Potential treasure.

effect.fleeting: The nest is dense but not uniform. Some areas are less webbed than others. Possible gaps.
effect.masterful: A narrow side passage. Spider-free but tight — single file. Bypasses the main nest entirely.
effect.legendary: Complete nest map: main passages, spider density, and two bypass routes. The shorter one is tight but safe. The longer one leads past something interesting.

scenes.title: The Threshold
scenes.readAloud:
Past the Nest, a final passage descends to an ancient doorway sealed with geometric patterns matching the exterior. This door hasn't opened in millennia.

The patterns pulse with that same sickly green light. But here, you can feel something behind the door — not malice, but... knowledge. Vast, cold, and indifferent. Whatever civilization built this place left something behind. Something that Revan took and used to win a war.

The door has no handle. No mechanism. Just patterns.
scenes.gmNotes: The sealed doorway to the Web of the Revanchist — the deepest chamber. The door requires a specific interaction: touching the geometric patterns in a sequence that represents a fundamental truth. Revan solved it by expressing 'sacrifice for the greater good.' The dark side corruption means the door also accepts darker truths — 'power through strength' or 'the ends justify the means.' The truth chosen affects what the Holocron reveals in Part 3.

decisions.choice: Express a truth of sacrifice and service
decisions.consequence: The Jedi approach. Door opens. Holocron provides clean navigational data.

decisions.choice: Express a truth of pragmatic necessity
decisions.consequence: The Revan approach. Door opens. Holocron provides data but with dark side taint — visions of the cost.
Bypass the philosophical test. Door opens but the Holocron's security activates more aggressively in Part 3.

narrativeLinks.note: The door opens to the Web of the Revanchist.

environmentMechanics.name: Philosophical Lock
environmentMechanics.effect: The door reads emotional and philosophical states, not physical input. Must express a genuine belief.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Authenticity. The door senses deception.

rewards.narrative: The door to the Web of the Revanchist opens. The Holocron awaits.

pacing.openingBeat: The sealed door. Patterns pulsing. Something beyond.
pacing.risingAction: Understanding the mechanism. What does it want?
pacing.climax: The truth expressed. The door responds.
pacing.resolution: The door opens. The final chamber.

disciplineChallenges.context: Expressing a fundamental truth to the doorway mechanism.

control.failure: The door rejects your expression. The patterns scramble. Try a different approach — force, intellect, or a deeper truth.
control.mastery: Your truth resonates so deeply that the door opens fully AND additional chambers unlock. The Spire responds to genuine conviction.

effect.fleeting: The patterns shift in response to your words. Partial alignment. The door cracks open — barely. Squeeze through.
effect.masterful: The patterns align. The door opens. Your truth is accepted. Full passage granted.
effect.legendary: The patterns blaze with light. Your truth was exactly what the builders intended. The door doesn't just open — the entire corridor illuminates, guiding you forward.

disciplineChallenges.context: Deciphering the pattern logic — what sequence opens the door.

control.failure: The logic is alien. Trial and error only. Each wrong attempt takes time.
control.mastery: Pattern decoded AND you understand the Spire's philosophical framework. All future puzzles in the structure are easier — the design language is consistent.

effect.fleeting: Not random. There's a mathematical component — base-12 geometry. But the organizing principle is still unclear.
effect.masterful: The patterns represent philosophical concepts. Identify the 'truth' the door expects. A puzzle, not a lock.
effect.legendary: Complete decryption: the patterns encode a question about the nature of knowledge — who deserves access to dangerous information? Answer the philosophy and the engineering follows.

disciplineChallenges.context: Brute-forcing the ancient mechanism with modern tools.

control.failure: Mechanism resists. Fusion Cutter can force it but triggers defensive protocols in the next chamber. Trade-off.
control.mastery: Clean mechanical bypass AND you preserve the mechanism intact. Can be re-sealed behind you — useful if something is following.

effect.fleeting: Modern tools create sparks on ancient alloy. The mechanism shifts reluctantly. Partial opening — enough to squeeze through.
effect.masterful: Door opens mechanically. Bypasses the philosophical test. The Spire doesn't object — apparently, ingenuity is also an acceptable answer.
effect.legendary: You interface modern tech with ancient engineering so elegantly that the mechanism responds as if designed for it. The door opens cleanly and the internal systems welcome you.

scenes.title: The Web of the Revanchist
scenes.readAloud:
The chamber beyond is breathtaking. A vast, spherical room carved from living crystal — the petrified heartwood of the massive tree. The walls are covered in a three-dimensional star map, holographic routes glowing between crystal nodes.

At the center, floating on nothing, is a Holocron. It's not a cube like the Jedi use — it's a polyhedron, obsidian black with gold geometric lines. Revan's personal Holocron.

As you approach, the Holocron activates. A holographic figure appears — a robed man with his face hidden behind a distinctive mask.

"I am the echo of Revan. I hold the paths between the stars. What do you seek?"
scenes.gmNotes: The Web of the Revanchist is Revan's greatest creation — a three-dimensional hyperspace map of the Unknown Regions. The Holocron at its center is keyed to Revan's echo (a gatekeeper AI). The Holocron contains the navigational data the heroes need for the Unknown Regions, but accessing it requires answering Revan's questions. The echo is not hostile — it's testing. Revan wanted the data to be used by someone worthy. The answers affect what data is revealed.

npcs.name: Echo of Revan
npcs.notes: Holographic AI with Revan's personality. Tests seekers before granting knowledge.

passive.name: Echo Chamber
passive.description: Cannot be harmed physically. Can only be interacted with socially or via the Force. If the Holocron is destroyed, the data is lost forever.

actions.name: Question
actions.description: Ask a philosophical question. The hero must answer with Insight or Resolve.

actions.name: Reveal
actions.description: Share one piece of navigational data. Requires a correct or honest answer first.

maneuver.name: Project Star Map
maneuver.description: Display a section of the Unknown Regions map on the chamber walls.

exploit.name: Full Download
exploit.description: If the heroes pass 3 questions, Revan grants full access to the navigational data.

threatBuild.notes: Not a combatant. An intelligence test. The Echo respects honesty over correctness. Revan valued pragmatism and sacrifice — answers that reflect those values earn access.

scenes.hazards: Holocron security. Force-reactive chamber. The dark side may interfere with the download.

decisions.consequence: The correct approach. Each honest answer unlocks more data.

decisions.choice: Try to take the Holocron by force
decisions.consequence: The chamber activates defensive protocols. Ancient security attacks.

decisions.choice: Use Tech to extract the data directly
decisions.consequence: Possible but the Holocron resists digital intrusion. Partial data at best.

environmentMechanics.name: Living Crystal Chamber
environmentMechanics.effect: The chamber amplifies Force abilities and emotions. Force-sensitive characters feel everything more intensely.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Focus. The amplification can be used constructively (Denia's guidance is clearer here).

environmentMechanics.name: Holocron Security
environmentMechanics.trigger: If heroes attempt force or digital intrusion
environmentMechanics.effect: The chamber's crystal nodes fire energy pulses. [Hazard 2] per round until intrusion stops.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Stop trying to force it. Answer the questions.

items.name: Revan's Holocron (Navigational Data)
items.note: Unknown Regions hyperspace routes. The key to reaching Leviathan.

rewards.narrative: Revan's navigational data acquired. The path to the Unknown Regions is open.

pacing.openingBeat: The crystal chamber. The star map. The Holocron.
pacing.risingAction: Revan's echo appears. The questions begin.
pacing.climax: The third question — "Why should I trust you?"
pacing.resolution: Data downloaded. The Holocron dims. Time to leave.

disciplineChallenges.context: Revan's first question: "What is worth dying for?"

control.failure: A glib or dishonest answer. Revan is disappointed. 'Try again.' Must answer genuinely or leave empty-handed.
control.mastery: Revan is moved. 'That answer... was my answer once.' A moment of connection across millennia. First lock opens AND Revan shares a personal memory.

effect.fleeting: An honest but simple answer. Revan considers it. The lock clicks, barely. 'Adequate.'
effect.masterful: An honest answer rooted in personal experience. Revan nods. First lock opens. 'You have thought about this.'
effect.legendary: An answer that surprises Revan. Silence. Then: 'No one has answered it that way before.' First lock opens wide. A deeper chamber is revealed.

disciplineChallenges.context: Revan's second question: "What is the difference between sacrifice and waste?"

control.failure: Revan challenges the answer. Must try another approach. 'You confuse one for the other. Think again.'
control.mastery: Revan reveals a personal regret: he made a sacrifice that was actually waste — and it cost him everything. The Fall began with a 'sacrifice' that wasn't. Lore revelation.

effect.fleeting: A philosophical answer. Revan tilts his head. Second lock clicks. 'Perhaps. We will see.'
effect.masterful: A thoughtful answer that demonstrates understanding. Second lock opens. 'You know the difference. That is rare.'
effect.legendary: An answer that stops Revan mid-response. He reconsiders. 'You understand it better than I did. That is... unexpected.' Second lock opens fully.

disciplineChallenges.context: Revan's third question: "Why should I trust you with the paths between the stars?"

control.failure: Partial access. Some routes are withheld. Must be supplemented with Adv5 drift algorithms. Revan doesn't fully trust you.
control.mastery: Full access AND Revan grants a final gift: the coordinates of his personal cache — equipment and writings hidden elsewhere in the Unknown Regions.

effect.fleeting: A sincere answer. Revan weighs it. Partial access granted. 'You are not ready for all of it. But you may have... some.'
effect.masterful: Full access granted. Navigational data downloaded. Revan: 'Use them wisely. The paths between the stars do not forgive mistakes.'
effect.legendary: Revan is satisfied. 'I have waited thousands of years for that answer.' Full access — every chart, every route, every annotation. The Spire's library opens completely.

disciplineChallenges.context: Studying the three-dimensional star map for Project Leviathan clues.

control.failure: The map is vast. Need more time to analyze. Preliminary findings only.
control.mastery: Route identified AND you notice the route has been recently accessed — energy patterns show someone queried this specific path within the last few years. The Empire has been here.

effect.fleeting: Hundreds of routes visible. Overwhelming. One cluster seems to point toward a heavily shielded region deeper in the Unknown Regions.
effect.masterful: One route leads to the Xala system — deep in the Unknown Regions. The Empire has been using this route. Project Leviathan connection confirmed.
effect.legendary: Full Unknown Regions navigation picture: the Xala system route, three alternative paths, and the specific stellar drift data needed to keep the routes current. The puzzle pieces are falling into place.

scenes.title: The Collapse
scenes.readAloud:
As you secure the data, the Spire shudders. Dust falls from the crystal ceiling. A deep, grinding sound echoes from below — something structural is failing.

Revan's echo speaks one last time: "The dark was never mine. It was already here. And now that you've taken what I left... it wants what's yours."

The crystals in the walls begin to crack. Green energy arcs between the fractures. The Spire is collapsing — or something is waking up.

Run.
scenes.gmNotes: The extraction of the Holocron data destabilizes the dark side nexus. The Spire begins to collapse. The heroes must escape vertically — back through the Nest (spiders are panicking and fleeing too), up through the vision chambers (no visions this time — too chaotic), and out through the entrance. Time pressure: 10 rounds to reach the surface before the Spire collapses entirely. Environmental hazards every round: falling stone, energy arcs, collapsing passages.
scenes.hazards: Structural collapse. Falling stone. Energy discharges. Panicking spiders blocking passages.

decisions.consequence: Fastest route. Athletics checks every round. Damage from near-misses.

decisions.choice: Take a safer but slower route
decisions.consequence: Less damage but risk being trapped by collapses.

environmentMechanics.name: Structural Collapse
environmentMechanics.effect: Falling stone in random zones. [Hazard 2]. Passages may become blocked.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Evasion. Move fast. Don't stop.

environmentMechanics.name: Energy Discharges
environmentMechanics.effect: Green energy arcs from cracking crystals. [Hazard 1] in random zones.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Stay away from walls. Move through center of passages.

environmentMechanics.name: 10-Round Timer
environmentMechanics.effect: Heroes have 10 rounds to reach the surface. Round 10 = total collapse.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Sprint. Help fallen allies. Don't get trapped.

rewards.narrative: Escape from the collapsing Spire. The heroes emerge into the dead forest.

pacing.openingBeat: The Spire shudders. Revan's warning. RUN.
pacing.risingAction: Vertical escape. Stone falling. Energy arcing. Spiders panicking.
pacing.climax: The final stretch — the entrance is visible but collapsing.
pacing.resolution: Burst into daylight. The Spire collapses behind you.

disciplineChallenges.context: Sprinting up the spiral passage as it collapses.

control.failure: Stumble. Falling stone forces a detour. Additional time in the collapse zone. Dangerous.
control.mastery: Sprint so fast you reach the exit first AND can hold a structural beam for others to pass under. Heroic moment.

effect.fleeting: Legs burning. Stone crashing behind you. You stay ahead of the worst of it but it's close.
effect.masterful: Full speed. Gain a round on the collapse. Clear of the danger zone before the ceiling comes down.
effect.legendary: Superhuman sprint through the disintegrating Spire. You vault fallen columns, slide under collapsing arches, and hit the exit at full speed.

disciplineChallenges.context: Dodging falling stone and energy arcs.

control.failure: Glancing hit from debris. Minor damage. Keep running — stopping is death.
control.mastery: You dodge debris AND identify the collapse pattern. Call out warnings to the team — no one else takes hits.

effect.fleeting: Stone clips your shoulder. Hurts but nothing's broken. Keep moving.
effect.masterful: Clean dodge. Stone crashes where you were standing a second ago. Not a scratch.
effect.legendary: You read the collapse like a language — predicting where each piece falls before it drops. Threading through a rain of stone without touching a single fragment.

disciplineChallenges.context: Maintaining pace despite exhaustion and injuries.

control.failure: Flagging. Another hero must help or you fall behind. The collapse doesn't wait.
control.mastery: Not only maintain pace but carry someone else's gear. The team moves as fast as the fastest member, not the slowest.

effect.fleeting: Every muscle screams. You're running on willpower alone. But you're still running.
effect.masterful: Push through. Keep running. The body wants to quit but the mind says no.
effect.legendary: Second wind. Something unlocks — adrenaline, Force, or pure stubbornness — and you're running faster than when you started.

disciplineChallenges.context: Identifying the safest route through the collapsing structure.

control.failure: Take the obvious route. More exposure to falling stone. Higher risk for everyone.
control.mastery: Spot the stable passage AND recognize that the collapse is following a pattern — the Spire is shutting down systematically, not randomly. The exit sequence is predictable.

effect.fleeting: One route looks slightly more stable than the others. Trust your gut. It holds — barely.
effect.masterful: Spot a stable passage. Avoid the worst collapses. The team takes the safest path out.
effect.legendary: You read the Spire's structural failure like a demolition expert. Lead the team through the one corridor that won't collapse for another 60 seconds. Perfect exit.

scenes.title: The Aftermath
scenes.readAloud:
You emerge into gray light and cold air. Behind you, the Ebon Spire — the greatest petrified tree in the forest — groans, cracks, and collapses inward. Millennia of dark side energy dissipate in a shockwave that makes every Force-sensitive in the sector flinch.

The dead forest is already changing. Where petrified stone stood, you see the first green shoots pushing through the gray soil. The death grip is broken.

Denia's voice reaches you — weak but warm: "I felt it. You did it. Come home."

Varth's voice follows, already calculating: "I've analyzed the navigational data. Combined with the drift algorithms from Malpaz... we can reach the Unknown Regions. We can find Leviathan."

A pause.

"But first, we need those algorithms. And the Empire is already there."
scenes.gmNotes: Denouement. The Spire is destroyed. The dark side nexus is broken — the dead forest begins to regrow. Rewards are distributed. Denia is strengthened by the dark side's dispersal. Varth reveals the next step: the Holocron data is incomplete without the drift algorithms from Malpaz (Adventure 5). The Ewoks may celebrate the heroes if the alliance was formed. The adventure ends with the ship lifting off from Endor — the journey into Act II's heart continues.

npcs.name: Ewok Celebration
npcs.notes: If the Ewok alliance was formed, Chirpa's village celebrates the destruction of the dead zone. Feast and gifts.

passive.name: Celebration
passive.description: Provides rest, healing, and gifts. Social scene.

actions.name: Feast
actions.description: Share food and drink. Remove [Fatigued] from all heroes.

threatBuild.notes: Non-combat social event. Optional based on Ewok alliance.

scenes.hazards: Shockwave from the Spire's collapse. Minor — more atmospheric than dangerous.

decisions.choice: Return to the Ewok village to celebrate
decisions.consequence: Rest, healing, and gifts. Ewok allies for possible future aid.
Faster. Varth is eager to move on. Skip the social reward.

narrativeLinks.note: The drift algorithms on Malpaz are the next objective.

environmentMechanics.name: Dark Side Dispersal
environmentMechanics.effect: The oppressive dark side aura is gone. [Rattled] and [Corrupted] conditions from the Descent fade. Force-sensitive characters feel relief.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: N/A — beneficial.

items.name: Revan's Holocron Data
items.note: Unknown Regions navigational routes. Incomplete without Malpaz drift algorithms.

items.name: Revan's Star Charts (partial)
items.note: Supplementary navigation data from the Spire walls.

items.name: Ewok Alliance Token
items.note: If alliance formed — carved wooden token. Ewoks will aid the heroes if they return to Endor.

items.name: [Clarity] bonus
items.note: Heroes who passed the Dark Reflections carry +1 Power for one more adventure.

rewards.narrative: The Ebon Spire is gone. The dark side nexus is broken. The navigational data is secured. Next stop: Malpaz — to steal the drift algorithms from the Empire.

pacing.openingBeat: Emergence into gray light. The Spire collapses.
pacing.risingAction: Green shoots in the dead forest. The death grip breaks.
pacing.climax: Varth's analysis: "We need the drift algorithms from Malpaz."

disciplineChallenges.context: Analyzing the complete navigational data with Varth.

control.failure: Data is dense. Varth needs time. Analysis continues in transit. Preliminary picture only.
control.mastery: Clear picture AND you identify encrypted Imperial navigation buoys along the route. The Empire has been using these lanes — and you now know their beacon frequencies.

effect.fleeting: The data is enormous. Initial analysis confirms: viable routes exist but they need stellar drift calibration to be safe.
effect.masterful: Clear picture: the Unknown Regions route is viable but needs drift algorithms from Malpaz to be safe. The final piece of the puzzle.
effect.legendary: Complete analysis: multiple viable routes, fallback paths, supply depot locations along the way, and — critically — the route the Empire is already using. You know where they're going.

disciplineChallenges.context: Reading Varth's reaction to the Holocron data — what is he planning?

control.failure: Varth seems appropriately professional. Nothing unusual. The mask holds.
control.mastery: Varth is excited about power AND you catch a microexpression when he looks at Denia — calculation. He's assessing her value as an asset. Again.

effect.fleeting: Varth is more energized than usual. Could be mission success high. Could be something else.
effect.masterful: He's excited. Too excited. He sees this data as his key to Leviathan — and to power. The professional mask slips.
effect.legendary: Complete read: Varth isn't celebrating the mission's success. He's celebrating his own. This data brings him closer to something personal — power, revenge, or redemption. He hasn't decided which.

disciplineChallenges.context: Celebrating with the Ewoks (if alliance exists).

control.failure: Polite farewell. No special gifts. The Ewoks are glad you survived but it's a brief parting.
control.mastery: Full celebration AND Chirpa offers a formal alliance. The Ewoks of Endor are allies of the heroes — a thread that echoes through the remaining campaign.

effect.fleeting: Warm goodbye. The Ewoks wave from the canopy as you return to your ship. Friendly but brief.
effect.masterful: Full celebration. Gifts: Ewok-crafted equipment, medicinal herbs, and a carved token of alliance. Real friends made.
effect.legendary: A night of celebration under the canopy. Songs, feasts, gifts, and a ceremony where Chirpa formally names the heroes 'friends of the tribe.' You depart with allies, equipment, and a warm memory.
